so at this point the director started going crazy

```
{
  "current_status_summary": "Shifting from one-way asymmetric communication to two-way Cut-and-Paste (Splicing) bounds. Implementing interactive TM crossing sequences to enforce algorithmic-agnostic limits on arbitrary 1D head movements.",
  "acknowledged_critic_fallacies": "The one-way communication assumption explicitly violates Algorithmic Agnosticism. TMs dynamically cross boundaries and can interleave reads.   ```
  ```

  as you can see this is a known LLM phenomenon called Token Collapse (or a Degeneration Loop), and it happens for a very specific reason in structured JSON environments.

  i asked gemini 0 and it said to change the temps of the models.
  i was using the default 1 but it told me to:
  set director 0.2
  set critc 0.3

  now the output is in the normal director.json file in this folder.
  
  and i added a new rule to the director and critc to always format the math in latex and not unicode symbols

  CRITICAL JSON RULE: When writing mathematical symbols or Lean 4 code inside the JSON values, you must use standard LaTeX (e.g., \\rightarrow, \\Omega) or standard ASCII. Do not use raw multi-byte Unicode math characters, as it will corrupt the JSON output.

  i also inputted it into the solvers and the verifer.
